title: rootless: support keep-id with one mapping
body:
support using keep-id when only one mapping is available to the rootless user.
    
When there is only one id available (e.g. there are no additional IDs set in /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid for the unprivileged user), then only add the identity mapping $ID -> $ID, leaving unmapped other IDs in the user namespace.
    
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] it needs a configuration with only one ID available.

```release-note
Now --userns=keep-id can be used when only one ID is available.
```

can you please add the lgtm label?

Question from the peanut gallery: Does this imply that I won't need an entry in `/etc/sub{uid,gid}` if I want to rootlessly run a simple container that only runs one thing, such as [Butane](https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-coreos/producing-ign/#_getting_butane)?
No,  You can run containers without /etc/subuid, /etc/subgid, iff you want to squash them into a single uid, but setting the ignore chown flag in storage.conf.
